core_mechanism: A two-sided critical-threshold (error-catastrophe) phase transition in coupled mutation-selection replicator dynamics: crossing a critical copying-error/mutation rate destroys a population's localization around its optimal type, and coupling two competing self-replicating populations creates a second, opposing threshold so the viable rate is confined to a finite window bounded above and below.
brief_reason: The abstract describes two coupled quasispecies with the classical error catastrophe at high mutation rates plus a second 'adaptation' catastrophe at low rates, i.e. a mutation-selection balance with two critical thresholds bounding the allowed range of mutation rates.
